---
title: PRISMA acknowledgement and references
---

`prisma-flow` is an independent Python implementation for producing
PRISMA-style flow diagrams. It is not the PRISMA reporting guideline itself and
is not affiliated with or endorsed by the PRISMA Executive.

## What PRISMA is

PRISMA stands for **Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic reviews and
Meta-Analyses**. The official PRISMA website describes PRISMA as a reporting
guideline intended to improve reporting of systematic reviews and to guide
authors in reporting why a review was done, what methods were used, and what
results were found.

The current main guideline is **PRISMA 2020**. It includes the statement paper,
checklists, and template flow diagrams for original and updated reviews.

## Acknowledgement

The PRISMA 2020 reporting guideline, checklist, and flow diagram templates were
developed by the PRISMA 2020 authors and are maintained through the PRISMA
Executive. `prisma-flow` acknowledges that work and aims only to provide a
Python implementation for generating diagrams and validating count
relationships in software workflows.

When using `prisma-flow` outputs in reports, manuscripts, presentations, or
software documentation, cite the original PRISMA 2020 publications as the
source of the reporting guideline.

It is also appropriate to cite `prisma-flow` as the software used to generate
the diagrams or serialized flow data. The two citations serve different
purposes: PRISMA should be cited for the reporting guideline, and `prisma-flow`
may be cited for the software implementation.

## Citing prisma-flow

If `prisma-flow` helps produce figures, outputs, or data files used in your
work, please cite the version of the software you used. Citation metadata is
available in the repository
[`CITATION.cff`](https://github.com/osl-incubator/prisma-flow/blob/main/CITATION.cff).

Suggested wording:

```text
PRISMA flow diagrams were generated with prisma-flow and reported according to
the PRISMA 2020 statement.
```

## Recommended PRISMA citations

The PRISMA website recommends citing the journal articles presenting the
statement paper and the explanation and elaboration paper.

### Statement paper

Page MJ, McKenzie JE, Bossuyt PM, Boutron I, Hoffmann TC, Mulrow CD, et al.
The PRISMA 2020 statement: an updated guideline for reporting systematic
reviews. *BMJ*. 2021;372:n71. doi:
[10.1136/bmj.n71](https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.n71).

### Explanation and elaboration paper

Page MJ, Moher D, Bossuyt PM, Boutron I, Hoffmann TC, Mulrow CD, et al.
PRISMA 2020 explanation and elaboration: updated guidance and exemplars for
reporting systematic reviews. *BMJ*. 2021;372:n160. doi:
[10.1136/bmj.n160](https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.n160).

## Official resources

- [PRISMA website](https://www.prisma-statement.org/)
- [PRISMA 2020 overview](https://www.prisma-statement.org/prisma-2020)
- [PRISMA 2020 flow diagrams](https://www.prisma-statement.org/prisma-2020-flow-diagram)
- [Citing PRISMA 2020](https://www.prisma-statement.org/citing-prisma-2020)

## Flow diagram licensing

The official PRISMA 2020 flow diagram templates are distributed under the
[Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).
That license permits reuse and adaptation, including commercial use, provided
the original work is properly cited.
